decisions.summary: Test decision logging for MCP verification
decisions.reasoning: Testing the nexus MCP server to verify all functions work correctly. This is a test decision to ensure the logging mechanism captures decisions properly.

decisions.summary: Built retro liminal website with Next.js, TypeScript, and abstract non-conventional design
decisions.reasoning: Created a liminal space aesthetic website using Next.js App Router with TypeScript for type safety and modern React patterns. Chose to implement abstract, non-conventional design with CSS animations for floating shapes, glitch effects, scanlines, and a constantly rotating Teto pear SVG component. Used pure CSS instead of animation libraries to maintain the retro aesthetic and reduce dependencies. Component structure separates the Teto pear into its own client component while keeping the main page server-rendered for performance.

decisions.summary: Redesigned website to match vintage liminal corridor aesthetic with yellow/orange tones
decisions.reasoning: Updated the entire color scheme from blue to vintage yellow/orange/brown tones to match the reference image (1.webp) showing a classic liminal space corridor. Replaced custom SVG pear with user-provided Teto pear GIF. Added vintage photo effects including film grain animation, motion blur, vignette overlay, and blur filters throughout to create an authentic retro camera aesthetic. These changes transform the design from a modern abstract look to a nostalgic, eerie liminal space experience.

decisions.summary: Reduced text intensity for better visual balance
decisions.reasoning: User requested less intensive text. Reduced font size from 4rem to 2.5rem, lowered base opacity from 0.15 to 0.08, changed font-weight from bold to normal, increased blur from 0.5px to 0.8px, slowed glitch animation from 3s to 5s, and reduced glitch effect peak opacities from 0.6/0.7 to 0.25/0.3 with softer text-shadows.

decisions.summary: Increased pear rotation speed for more dynamic effect
decisions.reasoning: User requested faster spinning pear. Changed rotation animation duration from 8s to 3s, making the pear spin approximately 2.7x faster for a more energetic visual effect.

decisions.summary: Added Framer Motion for interactive animations and navigation
decisions.reasoning: Implemented Framer Motion to enhance the liminal space website with interactive elements. Created a navigation bar with animated buttons at the top, added hover/tap interactions to the Teto pear component with particle effects, and made all text elements and shapes interactive with smooth animations. This significantly improves user engagement while maintaining the retro aesthetic.

decisions.summary: Use hue-rotate filter for color cycling on click
decisions.reasoning: Implemented color changing on click using CSS hue-rotate filter with 6 color values (0°, 60°, 120°, 180°, 240°, 300°) cycling through the color spectrum. This approach preserves the existing animations and drop-shadow effects while allowing smooth color transitions.

decisions.summary: Enhanced pear color-changing system with visual feedback
decisions.reasoning:
Implemented a comprehensive color-changing feature for the pear with:
1. ColorScheme interface to manage hue, name, and glow colors
2. Six distinct color schemes (Golden, Lime, Emerald, Cyan, Violet, Magenta)
3. Click state tracking to trigger visual effects
4. Ripple effect that expands and fades on click with color-matched borders
5. Color name indicator that appears temporarily when clicking
6. Dynamic glow colors that match each color scheme

This provides rich visual feedback and makes the color-changing feature more engaging and noticeable compared to the previous simple hue-rotate implementation.
